evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: with a focus on testing, observability, and CI/CD
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Explicit focus on testing, observability, and CI/CD foregrounds engineering quality practice over shipping speed, matching rigour.
evidence.domain_risk: developer productivity, platform health, and leadership decision-making
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Metrics inform leadership decisions and platform health but carry no regulatory or financial-reporting consequence, matching moderate risk.
evidence.collaboration_width: Data Engineers; Product; Engineering; Data Science; Platform partners
evidence.data_team_maturity: support rotation for key datasets, pipelines, and analytical products
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: A formal support rotation for established datasets and pipelines indicates a large, specialized, governance-heavy function, matching mature maturity.
evidence.jd_authorship: key metrics for platform health, developer productivity, and ML/AI platform adoption
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: The JD names specific, detailed metric domains (platform health, developer productivity, ML/AI adoption) reflecting concrete hiring-manager knowledge of the role, matching hiring_manager authorship.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Help define and evolve key metrics for platform health, developer productivity, and ML/AI platform adoption
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The role serves internal platform and engineering stakeholders through metrics on internal systems, matching internal_data orientation.
evidence.autonomy_level: Partner with Data Engineers on upstream pipelines and collaborate with Product, Engineering, and Data Science to scope and deliver insights
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The framing around partnering and collaborating to 'scope and deliver' insights describes delivering defined work rather than setting direction, matching execution autonomy.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: build and maintain analytical data models… improve data quality, performance, and cost efficiency
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The focus on maintaining and improving quality, performance, and cost efficiency of existing models describes extending and scaling existing infrastructure, matching fix_scale.

evidence.ai_role: key metrics for platform health, developer productivity, and ML/AI platform adoption
evidence.ai_role_explanation: Mentions ML/AI platform adoption but focuses on metrics and tracking rather than building AI infrastructure or using AI coding tools.
evidence.testing_framing: with a focus on testing, observability, and CI/CD
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Focus on testing indicates ownership of quality practices and testing discipline.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: support rotation for key datasets, pipelines, and analytical products
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Support rotation signals operational reliability concerns and incident-response responsibilities rather than compliance/regulatory risk.
